capabilities.boundary: Dynamic branch functions can choose one, many, or no successor nodes; missing targets fail the run; checkpoint resume preserves the already-computed branch queue.
Join nodes run only after all predecessor nodes complete; failed parents prevent the join; edge insertion order remains stable for deterministic replay.
A branch may fan out to multiple targets; completed targets are not rerun after checkpoint resume; empty fan-out completes; target failure stops sibling successors.
Looping branches can exit naturally, and per-run max step limits prevent unbounded execution.
Completed step exports can be imported into another run, emit import/resume events, and continue from the exported queue.
Interrupted step exports accept explicit resume requests, emit resume events, and continue without rerunning the interrupted node.
Graph checkpoints persist state, queue, completed-node metadata, phase, pending interrupt, effects, runtime IDs, and config version for stable resume.
A compiled graph can be mounted as a same-state runnable node; nested events inherit runtime IDs and carry parent node/step metadata; child checkpoints stay scoped.
Node code can emit child events into the graph event stream with parent node/step metadata for delegated agent or tool work.
Failed or canceled nodes stop successor execution and produce terminal failed or canceled run events with step snapshots.

Graph Runnable invocation can explicitly enable parallel fan-out with a required state merge function. Ready fan-out targets run concurrently, receive the same base state, emit stable target-order evidence, merge results in target order, cancel siblings on failure, and preserve existing checkpoint/resume behavior by falling back to the sequential executor when checkpointing is active.

capabilities.offline_proof: gopact-ext: (cd agents/humanreview && go test -count=1 ./...) && (cd tests/agents && go test -count=1 ./...)
capabilities.boundary: gopact-ext provides a provider-neutral approval gate that maps typed graph state to a gopact.InterruptRecord, uses a default approval resume schema, preserves custom schema and metadata by defensive copy, resumes from step export or checkpoint without rerunning the gate, and has cross-module graph composition coverage.

Graph and compiled Runnable values support first-class JSON Schema guards for graph state, per-node input, per-node output, and resumed state boundaries; guards reuse the injected root JSONSchemaValidator and topology exports include defensive copies of schema contracts.

gopact-ext provides a provider-neutral leased background worker primitive that runs one job or bounded drains, applies retry/stop/dead-letter queue transitions, renews worker ownership leases, refuses commits after lease loss, exposes an in-process test queue, and records schedule verification evidence.

Graph and compiled Runnable values expose stable topology exports with virtual boundary nodes, node kinds, static edges, branch counts, join predecessors, and compiled max-step limits for diagrams, audits, and tooling.
